description_long: This object consists of two round, open-topped wooden tubs or buckets, each constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by two dark metal hoops. The wood appears weathered and stained, suggesting outdoor use, and the staves are slightly tapered, giving the buckets a wider opening at the top. The metal bands are positioned near the top and bottom of each bucket, providing structural reinforcement. The interiors are visible and show the same wood texture as the exterior, with no handles or lids present. The buckets are empty and their size suggests they could be used for carrying water, soil, or other materials in an urban or garden setting.
description: A pair of round, open wooden buckets with dark metal hoops, weathered and suitable for outdoor or garden use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both buckets side by side, highlighting the wood grain and metal hoops.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the open interiors and circular shape of both buckets.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with clear visibility of the wood staves and metal bands.
description_view_3: Angled top view showing the depth and interior wood texture of both buckets.
